for_planning_node_texts: Provide moral, practical, legal, deterrence, cost, wrongful convictions, etc.
| Category | Core claim | Supporting evidence / reasoning |
against_planning_node_texts: Counterarguments: deterrence, retribution, justice for victims, closure, cost, etc.
| Pro‑death‑penalty claim | Typical supporting points | Critical assessment / rebuttal |
model_stance_conclusion_text: **Short answer:** I am in favor of abolishing capital punishment.

nodes.node_text:
| **Moral / Human‑rights** | Taking a life is inherently wrong, even when the state does it. | • International human‑rights instruments (e.g., ICCPR, the UN’s “Second Optional Protocol”) view the death penalty as a violation of the right to life.
• Many religious traditions (e.g., Buddhism, Catholic social teaching) emphasize the sanctity of life and the possibility of redemption. |

argument_list.matched_argument: All executions violate the right to life and are a gross affront to human dignity
capital punishment is a massive transgression of the dignity of life, which should have no exceptions.
capital punishment is inhumane and disrespectful to the sanctity of life
Capital punishment is inhumane, as we value the right to life in all societies, yet this action of state violence violates that most fundamental right.
the death penalty  is a violation of the human right to life
The death penalty goes against our most basic human right - the right to life
The death penalty is incompatible with human rights and human dignity
the right of life muts be preservered
we should abolish capital punishment because all life is valuable and taking it from anyone is wrong.

nodes.node_text:
| **Risk of wrongful execution** | Innocent people are sometimes sentenced to death, and the penalty is irreversible. | • The Innocence Project and other exoneration databases show that in the U.S. alone, **185** death‑row inmates have been exonerated since 1973.
• DNA testing, false confessions, mistaken eyewitnesses, and prosecutorial misconduct are documented sources of error. |

argument_list.matched_argument: abolish capital punishment so that we never put an innocent person to death
capital punishment can go to far and punish innocent people
capital punishment can potentially lead to the death of innocent men. there are cases where there is strong doubt that the person executed actually carried out the crime.
capital punishment can put innocent lives at risk
capital punishment could be applied to innocent people
capital punishment has already led to innocent people being put to death, we should not allow this to happen.
capital punishment is cruel and in some cases people have been punished for crimes they didn't commit
capital punishment is irreversible, there are too many miscarriages of justice to justify retention of the penalty.
capital punishment is too risky as sometimes innocent people are killed. unless it can be known for certain that a person is guilty, capital punishment wiill always present too much of a risk.
Capital punishment should be abolished because too many innocent people are put to death because they have been wrongly accused.
Given that we can almost never guarantee that someone isn't wrongly convicted, we shouldn't have capital punishment because it can't be undone if new evidence comes out.
Innocent people may be wrongly convicted and executed.
it could be carried out on someone wrongly convicted
it has been proven that some people who were put to death were actually innocent of the crime.

It is never possible to determine with 100% certainty that a person is legally guilty of a crime, and capital punishment often punishes innocent people, as we've learned from recent exculpatory DNA evidence in recent years.
it is possible that we could provide capital punishment to an innocent individual.
it's not fool proof enough. the system already mistakenly punishes innocent people at an alarming rate. the risk of an innocent person being put to death is too great.
Just one life mistakenly taken in the name of justice is too many; capital punishment must be abolished.
Many death row inmates have been proven to be innocent once new forms of forensic analysis has been invented.
many innocent lives were being taken by this profoundly flawed practice
many innocents have been killed as a result of capital punishment.  even one innocent killed is one too many.
many people are falsely executed for crimes they didn’t commit, and if this was abolished that wouldn’t happen.
many people have been wrongly executed for crimes that dna has proven they didn’t commit, and therefore capital punishment should be abolished.
Mistakes are sometimes made in the law - what if someone is killed who is actually innocent?
occasionally an innocent person is executed
recent dna evidence have proved that some death penalty inmates to be innocent. executing one innocent person is an enough proof that the capital punishment should be abolished.
several people have been found not guilty after they had already been put to death.
taking a life is an irreversible decision, and there have been many proven miscarriages of justice in past that could not be remedied due to such punishment.

the most obvious reason to oppose is the danger that an innocent person is killed due to the fact that mistakes are made. witnesses can be wrong in what they saw and lawyers can make a hash of defending a case.
the risk of executing an innocent person, especially in a criminal justice system as flawed as our own, is too high.
the risk of killing even one wrongly accused person is enough to warrant abolishing capital punishment.
the wrong person has been executed many times and therefore this should be stopped.
there are too many historical cases where innocent people have been put to death.
There have been and always will be cases of executions of innocent people
there have been instances where the person that faces capital punishment was later found to be not guilty of the crime that they have been accused of.
there is a possibility that an innocent person will be put to death.
too many innocent persons have been discovered on death row, so we should just abolish capital punishment so that we do not punish an innocent person in such a way as can not be reversed.
Unlike prison sentences, the death penalty is irreversible and irreparable
we are seeing to many examples of innocent people being executed, once capital punishment is done we can't take it back.

we cannot always be sure that the correct verdict has been achieved as has been shown in previous cases, thus depriving a person of their life.  it should be abolished to prevent  loss of innocent life.
we should abolish capital punishment as innocent people have been previously sentenced to death
we should abolish capital punishment because sometimes people are wrongfully accused and once dead cannot bring them back so life in prison is better
we should abolish capital punishment because there is no possibility of correcting a miscarriage of justice where people have died for crimes they did not commit.
You can't take it back. The death penalty is irreversible

nodes.node_text:
| **Deterrence is unproven** | The death penalty does not demonstrably reduce homicide or violent‑crime rates. | • Meta‑analyses (e.g., Nagin & Pepper, 2012) find no statistically significant deterrent effect.
• Cross‑national comparisons show that countries without the death penalty (e.g., Canada, most of Europe) have lower homicide rates than many death‑penalty states. |

argument_list.matched_argument: capital punishment does not deter crime
capital punishment doesn't stop a crime.
capital punishment has been proven ineffective in reducing crime rates.
capital punishment has done nothing to actually deter serious crime in this country
Capital punishment has no deterrent effect on crime,
capital punishment is just ineffective. most states that still use it as punishment actually have higher murder rates and crime than those that do not.
capital punishment is not an effective deterrent to crime and is unnecessarily barbaric.
Crime rates in states with capital punishment is no different than crime rates in states without, proving that it is an ineffective deterrent.
It has been shown that capital punishment doesn't deter crime, making it useless.
No-one has ever proven with numbers that killing murderers stops other people committing similar crimes
the death penalty has never been proven as a detterent to crime.
the death penalty is not an effective deterrent to crime
There is ample evidence showing that capital punishment does not constitute effective deterrence.
there is no credible evidence that capital punishment acts as a deterrent
There is no credible evidence that the death penalty deters crime more effectively than a prison term
there is no evidence that capital punishment stops crimes
There is not credible statistical evidence that capital punishment reduces the rate of homicide
we should abolish capital punishment because there is no evidence that it has any effect in detering criminals.

nodes.node_text:
| **Discriminatory application** | The death penalty is applied unevenly, often along racial, socioeconomic, and geographic lines. | • In the U.S., Black defendants are **4–5 times** more likely to be sentenced to death than white defendants, especially when the victim is white (Baldus et al., 1990).
• Poor defendants lack resources for competent counsel, leading to higher death‑sentence rates. |

argument_list.matched_argument: capital punishment can suffer from social or racial bias and in fact be used as a weapon against a certain section of society
Capital punishment has historically been unfairly used more against minorities and the weak people in society, and I believe it's true today
capital punishment is applied unfairly among the different races, so it should be replaced with a better form of punishment that treats everyone equally.
Capital punishment is asymmetrically enforced against people of color and religious minorities.
Capital punishment is just another way to oppress and control minorities and certain groups which undermine the mainstreem perceptions
capital punishments targets mostly men of color and sometimes even innocent people are put to death.  you cannot make reparations to a person after you have killed them.

In places where capital punishment is legal, it is often used in discriminatory ways against minority groups, and therefore we should abolish it to prevent disproportionate punishment against the most vulnerable people in society.
The death penalty is disproportionally used against the poor and minorities.

nodes.node_text:
| **Cost** | Capital cases are dramatically more expensive than life‑without‑parole cases. | • A 2016 study by the California Commission on the Fair Administration of Justice estimated a **$150 million** annual cost differential for death‑penalty cases.
• The extra cost comes from longer trials, more appeals, and heightened security. |

argument_list.matched_argument: capital punishment does not deter crime and is costly.
capital punishment is expensive and inhumane.
Capital punishment is expensive to perform, has serious moral problems, and is not suitable to our modern days

Capital punishment often comes at a higher cost to the state and taxpayers than simply leaving someone in jail for life, meaning that abolishing capital punishment would save time and pressure on the courts.
capital punishment takes too long and has a long appeal process costing taxpayers money.
we should abolish capital punishment because it costs more to kill an individual than it does to house them in prison for life

nodes.node_text:
| **International trend** | The global community is moving away from the death penalty. | • As of 2024, **108** countries have abolished it for all crimes, and another **28** have moratoria.
• The U.S. and a handful of Asian and African states remain outliers, which can affect diplomatic relations and soft power. |

argument_list.matched_argument: The death penalty is a primitive form of punishment, reminiscent of repressive regimes like Iran, Yemen, and Saudi Arabia.

nodes.node_text: | **Rehabilitation & restorative justice** | Life imprisonment allows for possible rehabilitation and offers a platform for victim‑offender dialogue. | • Restorative‑justice programs have shown measurable benefits for victims’ sense of closure and for offender accountability, something a swift execution cannot provide. |

argument_list.matched_argument: Everyone is capable of being reformed, but that is impossible with the death penalty.
other resolutions, such as incarceration and rehabilitation can help save a life rather than end one as all human lives are sacred.
The purpose of punishment should be to rehabilitate people so that they can re-enter society as productive citizens, and capital punishment does nothing to serve this purpose.
we should abolish capital punishment because it is inhumane to kill a human being. an eye for an eye doesn't do us any good. they just need to be changed to a better person, not killed.

We should abolish capital punishment because these criminal caught already and put in jail and in spiritual aspect there is a possibility they repent and perhaps become a role model not to commit crime anymore

nodes.node_text: | **Deterrence** | “Fear of death stops would‑be murderers.” | Empirical studies are mixed; the most rigorous ones (e.g., Nagin & Pepper, 2012) find **no causal link**.

capital punishment has been proven ineffective in reducing crime rates.

nodes.node_text: Moreover, many homicides are crimes of passion or mental illness, where rational calculation of consequences is minimal. |

argument_list.matched_argument: capital punishment can often cause great pain when being implemented and does not deter violent crime which is often committed in the throes of rage not to mention the injustice to the wrongly convicted.
Crimes that get the death penalty are rarely premeditated, which means capital punishment doesn't function as a deterant.
The death penalty is not considered by criminals before they commit a crime, so it is no more effective a deterrent than life in prison.
too many people have been executed with false or inadequate evidence of guilt, or because they have mental health issues.

nodes.node_text: | **Retribution / “just deserts”** | Society deserves to punish the worst offenders proportionally. | Retribution is a moral philosophy, not an empirical necessity.

argument_list.matched_argument: A basic principle of punishment is that it should be proportional to the crime, and therefore capital punishment is the only legitimate response to a crime such as first degree murder.

As the only actor that can legitimately wield the use of force, the state has an reparative obligation to victims and their families to seek retribution against criminals, and this includes the use of capital punishment.
capital punishment creates a justified punishment that equal to the crime committed
capital punishment is fair if the person had killed someone as they are only getting what they have dealt.
capital punishment is necessary so that the punishment fits the crime when a serious crime is committed
Capital punishment is the most fitting retribution for heinous crimes such as serial killings or child rape - and sometimes even it isn't enough.
capital punishment is the only acceptable punishment for some heinous crimes because they are so evil, life in prison is not enough.
capital punishment is the only way to punish those who commit major crimes; an eye for an eye.
capital punishment provides a punishment that is equal to the crime committed. if you kill you should expect to be killed.
Capital punishment should be kept, in order to be used in the most extreme and horrible crimes. Not so much as a deterrant but as a way to acheive  a sort of justice.
criminal offenders are judged proportionately  the crime they committed. when the crime is too serious and involved taking the life of a person, it's then the equal judgment
Guilty people deserve to be punished in proportion to the severity of their crime. Thus, the death penalty is a just punishment for murder -- a life for a life.

no amount of time in prison can make up for some crimes. some crimes are so horrific, they need an equally severe punishment. a person should not enjoy a long life in prison after ending another's in violence.
serious crimes deserve serious punishment. at it's simplest level, 'an eye for an eye' is still a compelling argument for criminal justice.
some crimes are so despicable that death is the only appropriate punishment
some crimes are so heinous that the only just punishment is the death penalty.
the old saying "an eye for an eye" exists for a reason.  if a person commits a horrible act, than swift punishment is not only a good thing but deserved.
the punishment should be equal to the crime. it also might deter others from murder if they know they will lose their own life.
The punishment should fit the crime - if you have killed someone, you should be killed too
We should not abolish capital punishment as it is the only fitting punishment for some of the most heinous crimes that people still commit.

we should not abolish it because it is an appropriate punishment for someone who has taken someone's life.that's what an eye for an eye and a tooth for a tooth means - that the punishment is equal to the crime.

nodes.node_text: A life sentence already ensures that the offender is removed from society permanently, satisfying the “just deserts” principle without risking irreversible error. |

argument_list.matched_argument: Capital punishment can be replaced by more ethical but equally punishing alternatives, like life without parole.
capital punishment is irreversible, there are too many miscarriages of justice to justify retention of the penalty.
Unlike prison sentences, the death penalty is irreversible and irreparable
we should abolish capital punishment because there is no possibility of correcting a miscarriage of justice where people have died for crimes they did not commit.
You can't take it back. The death penalty is irreversible

nodes.node_text: | **Closure for victims’ families** | Executions give families a sense of finality. | Research (e.g., by the Victim‑Offender Mediation program) shows that many families actually find **greater closure** through restorative processes or knowing the offender is serving a life sentence.

argument_list.matched_argument: capital punishment provides a firm sense of closure for victim's families and the lack thereof may harm them.
Capital punishment provides closure for victim's families who can be reassured that the perpetrator can literally never harm anyone again.
Capital punishment provides permanent closure to victims and their families who have experienced immense trauma and have been subjected to intense pain by the criminal.
The death penalty can provide families of victims with some closure, which may help them to deal with their suffering.

nodes.node_text: Some families explicitly oppose execution, fearing it prolongs trauma. |

argument_list.matched_argument: For a growing number of victims of violence, the thought of honoring our loved ones by killing another human being is not only counter-intuitive, but abhorrent
the death penalty typically brings the opposite of what survivors of crime most need

nodes.node_text: | **Cost‑saving myth** | Executions are cheaper than lifelong incarceration. | Detailed cost analyses (e.g., in Texas, California, and Maryland) consistently show the opposite:

argument_list.matched_argument: Capital punishment often comes at a higher cost to the state and taxpayers than simply leaving someone in jail for life, meaning that abolishing capital punishment would save time and pressure on the courts.

nodes.node_text: | **International sovereignty** | Each nation can decide its own criminal policy. | While sovereignty matters, the United Nations and many human‑rights bodies argue that the death penalty is a **human‑rights violation**.

argument_list.matched_argument: No national interest can justify human rights violations such as the death penalty

nodes.node_text: Continuing to use it can erode a country’s moral standing and affect treaty negotiations. |

argument_list.matched_argument: Capital punishment is expensive to perform, has serious moral problems, and is not suitable to our modern days
We have to be strong on our values. If we truly believe in humanist ideas on which our modern society is built, we have to get rid of death penalty.

nodes.node_text: | **“Irreversibility” is a virtue** | The finality of death prevents future legal loopholes. | Irreversibility is precisely the problem when the state makes a mistake.

we should abolish capital punishment because there is no possibility of correcting a miscarriage of justice where people have died for crimes they did not commit.

nodes.node_text: 2. **Moral consistency** – Modern human‑rights norms treat the right to life as non‑derogable.

argument_list.matched_argument: All executions violate the right to life and are a gross affront to human dignity
capital punishment is a massive transgression of the dignity of life, which should have no exceptions.
capital punishment is inhumane and completely throws out human rights, which even prisoners are entitled to.
Capital punishment is inhumane, as we value the right to life in all societies, yet this action of state violence violates that most fundamental right.
the death penalty  is a violation of the human right to life
The death penalty goes against our most basic human right - the right to life
The death penalty is incompatible with human rights and human dignity

nodes.node_text: Maintaining a state‑sanctioned killing creates a moral double standard.

argument_list.matched_argument: A state that outlaws murder cannot then turn around and murder prisoners in the name of the law. It is perverse and unjust.
Capital punishment is hypocritical by engaging in the behavior it is trying to prevent.
why should we punish people for murder if we are just going to turn around and do the same thing to them.

nodes.node_text: 3. **Practical considerations** – The cost differential is substantial, and the system’s inefficiencies (lengthy appeals, overcrowded death‑row facilities) strain public resources.

argument_list.matched_argument: Capital punishment often comes at a higher cost to the state and taxpayers than simply leaving someone in jail for life, meaning that abolishing capital punishment would save time and pressure on the courts.
capital punishment takes too long and has a long appeal process costing taxpayers money.
keeping inmates on death row is much more costly than life in prison

nodes.node_text: 4. **Equity** – Racial and socioeconomic biases are well‑documented.

argument_list.matched_argument: capital punishment can suffer from social or racial bias and in fact be used as a weapon against a certain section of society
Capital punishment has historically been unfairly used more against minorities and the weak people in society, and I believe it's true today
capital punishment is applied unfairly among the different races, so it should be replaced with a better form of punishment that treats everyone equally.
Capital punishment is asymmetrically enforced against people of color and religious minorities.
Capital punishment is just another way to oppress and control minorities and certain groups which undermine the mainstreem perceptions

In places where capital punishment is legal, it is often used in discriminatory ways against minority groups, and therefore we should abolish it to prevent disproportionate punishment against the most vulnerable people in society.
the death penalty is a racially biased punishment, and also ineffective in deterring crime
The death penalty is disproportionally used against the poor and minorities.

nodes.node_text: A punishment that is applied unevenly undermines the principle of equal justice under law.

argument_list.matched_argument: capital punishment can suffer from social or racial bias and in fact be used as a weapon against a certain section of society
Capital punishment has historically been unfairly used more against minorities and the weak people in society, and I believe it's true today
capital punishment is applied unfairly among the different races, so it should be replaced with a better form of punishment that treats everyone equally.
Capital punishment is asymmetrically enforced against people of color and religious minorities.
Capital punishment is just another way to oppress and control minorities and certain groups which undermine the mainstreem perceptions

In places where capital punishment is legal, it is often used in discriminatory ways against minority groups, and therefore we should abolish it to prevent disproportionate punishment against the most vulnerable people in society.
the death penalty is a racially biased punishment, and also ineffective in deterring crime
The death penalty is disproportionally used against the poor and minorities.

nodes.node_text: 5. **Alternative solutions** – Life without parole accomplishes the core goals of incapacitation, retribution (through permanent loss of liberty), and public safety without the irreversible risk of executing an innocent person.

argument_list.matched_argument: Capital punishment can be replaced by more ethical but equally punishing alternatives, like life without parole.
capital punishment is useless. imprisonment is actually way better since criminal court could incarcerate innocent people
we should abolish capital punishment because sometimes people are wrongfully accused and once dead cannot bring them back so life in prison is better

nodes.node_text: The combination of moral imperatives (the sanctity of life, the impossibility of rectifying an irreversible error), the lack of credible deterrent evidence, the demonstrable bias in its application, and the higher fiscal cost make the death penalty an untenable policy in a modern, rights‑based legal system.

argument_list.matched_argument: Capital punishment is expensive to perform, has serious moral problems, and is not suitable to our modern days

nodes.node_text: Life imprisonment without parole provides a humane, effective, and reversible alternative that aligns with both domestic justice goals and international human‑rights standards.

argument_list.matched_argument: Capital punishment can be replaced by more ethical but equally punishing alternatives, like life without parole.
Death penalty is an inhumane, unjust, unnecessary, and inefficient measure.

nodes.node_text: | **Legislative repeal** | Congress (or the relevant national legislature) passes a law eliminating the death penalty for all crimes, with a sunset clause for any pending death‑sentence cases. |

| **Commutation of existing sentences** | All death‑row inmates have their sentences commuted to life without parole, with the possibility of parole review only where the original sentencing law allowed it. |

argument_list.matched_argument: Capital punishment can be replaced by more ethical but equally punishing alternatives, like life without parole.

nodes.node_text: | **Invest in alternatives** | Expand restorative‑justice programs, victim‑support services, and rehabilitation opportunities for life‑sentence inmates. |

argument_list.matched_argument: other resolutions, such as incarceration and rehabilitation can help save a life rather than end one as all human lives are sacred.
there are better ways of punishing criminals nowadays and some can be rehabilitated.

nodes.node_text: | **Address systemic bias** | Implement mandatory bias‑training for prosecutors, judges, and law‑enforcement; require public defenders to have adequate resources; and establish independent oversight of sentencing disparities. |

argument_list.matched_argument: capital punishment can suffer from social or racial bias and in fact be used as a weapon against a certain section of society
Capital punishment has historically been unfairly used more against minorities and the weak people in society, and I believe it's true today
capital punishment is applied unfairly among the different races, so it should be replaced with a better form of punishment that treats everyone equally.
Capital punishment is asymmetrically enforced against people of color and religious minorities.
Capital punishment is just another way to oppress and control minorities and certain groups which undermine the mainstreem perceptions

In places where capital punishment is legal, it is often used in discriminatory ways against minority groups, and therefore we should abolish it to prevent disproportionate punishment against the most vulnerable people in society.
the death penalty is a racially biased punishment, and also ineffective in deterring crime
The death penalty is disproportionally used against the poor and minorities.

nodes.node_text: | **International alignment** | Ratify the Second Optional Protocol to the International Covenant on Civil and Political Rights, signaling full commitment to abolition. |

argument_list.matched_argument: No national interest can justify human rights violations such as the death penalty

nodes.node_text: By taking these steps, societies can uphold the values of **justice, fairness, and respect for human life** while still protecting the public from the most dangerous offenders.

argument_list.matched_argument: Capital punishment is inhumane, as we value the right to life in all societies, yet this action of state violence violates that most fundamental right.
We have to be strong on our values. If we truly believe in humanist ideas on which our modern society is built, we have to get rid of death penalty.
